purpose: Recast $/MW-normalizable EXOGENOUS-wildfire benchmarks onto the canonical wildfire_solar failure-unit value buckets. It IS a grain/denominator reconciliation. It IS NOT a calibration verdict. Headline: no properly-scoped exogenous-wildfire $/MW severity exists to recast, and the DR ordinates that would place one are withheld upstream (NO_RUNTIME_CURVE).
headline: Across all 63 catalogued sources / 100 benchmark rows, ZERO isolated exogenous-wildfire solar $/MW physical-severity figures exist. All normalized_usd_per_MW cells are blank (never zero). The value grain is resolved from the canonical cell; DR ordinates are withheld (NO_RUNTIME_CURVE). Nothing valid to recast.

rows.interpretation: No open, properly-scoped exogenous-wildfire solar $/MW severity benchmark exists. Value grain resolved; DR withheld; nothing to recast.

ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY. ~1,000 of ~80,000 modules destroyed (~1% module-population burned fraction at this event) - a unit-count anchor for WS_MODULE_THERMAL, NOT a $/MW severity and NOT a DR ordinate. Fire + firefighting-water pathways blended.
Vendor-reported avoided-cost/restoration-savings (PORTFOLIO_BIAS), not gross incurred damage. Not normalizable; context only.

guardrails: Blanks not zeros: a missing $/MW is left null, never 0.
A value share (bucket) is a denominator, not a damage ratio. DR ordinates are withheld (NO_RUNTIME_CURVE).
Keep endogenous, all-cause-blended, and smoke/soiling numbers OUT of this crosswalk - they are not exogenous-wildfire physical severity.
This crosswalk organizes and normalizes; it does not validate or calibrate anything.
